title: _io: StringIO iteration (tp_iternext) is unlocked and races with seek() on self->pos
description: Every StringIO method impl is @critical_section-wrapped, but the hand-written tp_iternext slot stringio_iternext takes no critical section: it calls _stringio_readline, which reads/advances self->pos (and touches self->buf) unlocked. Iterating a shared StringIO (readlines()/list()/for-line-in) therefore races with a concurrent seek()/write()/truncate() that DOES hold the lock.
sites: Modules/_io/stringio.c _stringio_readline:365 (read self->pos >= self->string_size; NO critical section)
Modules/_io/stringio.c _stringio_readline:383 (write self->pos += len; NO critical section)
Modules/_io/stringio.c stringio_iternext:421 (calls _stringio_readline WITHOUT Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION; tp_iternext slot wired at :1091)
race_kind: half-locked object: methods @critical_section-protected but tp_iternext slot unlocked (missing per-object critical section)
shared_state: stringio.pos (Py_ssize_t stream position); the same unlocked slot also exposes stringio.buf / stringio.string_size
severity: low as observed (value-benign race on aligned self->pos, no crash), medium latent (unlocked iterator vs a lock-holding truncate()/write() -> resize_buffer/PyMem_Realloc frees self->buf -> potential UAF/OOB '\0' write in _stringio_readline)
suggested_fix: wrap stringio_iternext's body in Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(self)/Py_END_CRITICAL_SECTION() (around the _stringio_readline call), matching every other StringIO entry point. Put the section in the unlocked caller, NOT in the shared helper _stringio_readline, which is also called from the already-locked _io_StringIO_readline_impl (critical sections are not recursive).
notes: Real FT-safety gap, not 'don't share this object' user error: CPython deliberately @critical_section-annotated every StringIO method to make concurrent use data-race-free, and the tp_iternext slot was simply overlooked. Fleet seed caught the read/write variant (_stringio_readline:365 vs seek:543); the minimized repro caught the write/write variant (_stringio_readline:383 vs seek:543) -- same self->pos field, same two functions. Audit other _io types / any @critical_section-hardened type for hand-written slots (tp_iternext etc.) that bypass the section.

related_issues.note: Use-after-free with concurrent stringio_iternext — same bug
PR wraps stringio_iternext in Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION — exactly the fix

assessment: DUPLICATE of upstream #153296 (fix in PR #153368) — already reported
